title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ has a clique or stable set of cardinality at least $|G|^\varepsilon$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is the central motivation for this paper. The specific open case of $H = P_5$ (equivalently, $H$ = house) is described as one of the three smallest open cases and has been the subject of much study. The house is the smallest hole-with-hat.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the class of hole-with-hat-free graphs: there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every hole-with-hat-free graph $G$ has a clique or stable set of cardinality at least $|G|^\varepsilon$. This strictly generalises the house-free case (since hole-with-hat-free implies house-free) but does not resolve the house/$P_5$ open case.
notes: The house/$P_5$ special case is explicitly flagged as still open in the abstract. The paper text is truncated after Section 3; any conjectures posed in later sections are not visible in the provided extract.
